page: Amazon Web Services m6id.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is provided via 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with the Passmark CPU score, extended instructions, and encryption tests ranking in the top 10% tier. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks place in the top 25%, while single-core performance generally falls into the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with uncached large-block reads reaching 339.2 GB/sec and writes at 133.21 GB/sec. The system also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in image processing and ray tracing, alongside strong database operations and Redis SET throughput.

Given its bare-metal architecture and high resource density, the m6id.metal offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring direct access to physical hardware without hypervisor overhead. The combination of massive local NVMe storage and high memory capacity makes it well-suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy machine learning training, though it maintains reasonable CPU-based LLM inference speeds for prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.metal is a bare-metal general-purpose server configured with 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) from dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top tier for Passmark CPU scores, encryption, and ray tracing, with strong multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The server's high-density physical resources and direct hardware access provide qualitative cost efficiency for demanding enterprise applications. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive data processing workloads that benefit from local NVMe instance storage without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and encryption performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal server combining dedicated multi-core Intel processors with massive local NVMe storage for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
512.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in Passmark CPU, encryption, and ray tracing benchmarks
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfers
Bare-metal architecture offering direct hardware access without hypervisor overhead
Optimized for large-scale databases, software compilation, and web serving
